body.title: CC shim: priority/HALT message path — any bus device can interrupt CC
body.result: priority/HALT envelopes → _fire_interrupt(); 3x Enter + message body; 4 tests; 19 pass
body.description:
**Problem:** There's no mechanism for any device (or Akien via web UI) to send an urgent interrupt to CC. HALT-class messages currently don't exist.

**What to build:** In CCWorkerListener, handle envelopes with kind='priority' or kind='halt'. On receipt, immediately fire the interrupt sequence to the CC tmux session: `tmux send-keys -t <session> Enter Enter Enter <message_body> Enter`. This bypasses the nag interval entirely and fires synchronously. Any device on the bus can send this — Granny, Igor, Akien's web UI, anything.

HALT payload shape: `{ 'kind': 'halt', 'message': '<human-readable reason>' }` — the message body is injected as-is into CC's session.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/cc_worker_listener.py
**Design rules:** Priority path is synchronous — no queueing, fires immediately on receipt
**Scope boundary:** Receive and inject. Does not define who is allowed to send (trust/auth is out of scope here).
**Completion criteria:** Test shows: priority envelope arrives → tmux interrupt sequence fires immediately with the message body. HALT envelope does the same.
